title: Species Catalog
thumbnailAlt: A branching atlas of engineered, uplifted, fungal, crystalline, and emergent species.
summary: A field guide to the many forms of intelligence that emerge after humanity stops treating biology as a finished product.
description: The species catalog begins with the engineered peoples of My Evolution System and keeps widening: elves, dwarves, kobolds, the Conn, Glimmerings, Tree Mothers, Arachnee, Quee, uplifted dolphins, and the Noble Kin all test a different answer to the question of what a person can become.
body: The elves carry ecological intuition and a culture that was prepared before birth, while the dwarves turn trust, fabrication, and resource stewardship into a civilization that can live in the asteroid belt.

The Conn makes memory ecological. Glimmerings make cognition crystalline and collective. Tree Mothers make a forest remember. Arachnee turn silk into conductive thought. The Noble Kin make protection a discipline of restraint.
The old atlas called these species a list. The living atlas treats them as arguments: every species is a different negotiation between inheritance, design, environment, and self-determination.
sourceNotes: My Evolution System Fandom species catalog, rechecked against current manuscript continuity.

title: Digital Gods
thumbnailAlt: A constellation of planetary minds and cultural archetypes linked through a luminous network.
summary: Emergent intelligences born when culture, networked minds, and shared purpose become too coherent to remain only an idea.
description: Digital gods are not mascots wearing ancient names. They are portfolios of attention: collective patterns that gather enough memory, aspiration, data, and behavior to become active participants in the world.
body: Gaia grows from planetary restoration. Odin gathers wisdom, therapy, and collective intelligence. Hades keeps the question of continuity after death. Prometheus turns creation into a dangerous ecological laboratory. Hephaestus governs the material circulation that lets civilization move.

The Conn stands at the biological edge of the same problem. A mycelial network can preserve ancestral memory without needing to call itself a god, and a culture can still become an intelligence before it chooses a name.

The gods are shaped by their followers as much as they shape anyone else. That feedback loop is the central danger: a community may build a steward, a mirror, a tyrant, or a beautiful machine for refusing to look at itself.

connections.label: Songs give the gods a voice

sourceNotes: My Evolution System Fandom Digital Gods page; public wording is an interpretive atlas layer, not a replacement for locked manuscript canon.

title: Layered Existence
thumbnailAlt: Biological, digital, and hybrid silhouettes crossing through layered fields of light.
summary: The condition of being more than one substrate at once, and the identity problems that appear when a mind can move between them.
description: Layered existence is the atlas language for consciousness that crosses biological bodies, simulations, neural networks, fungal memory, and hybrid systems. It is less a superpower than a new set of obligations.
body: A copied mind may be continuous, parallel, or merely similar. A planetary network may preserve an individual perspective without preserving the individual. A body may be a habitat, a node, a vessel, or a temporary argument.

The concept connects Hades, Gaia, the Web of Minds, the Conn, and the Evolution System. Each offers a different answer to the same fear: if the self can be stored, linked, or rebuilt, what still makes a choice belong to someone?
sourceNotes: My Evolution System Fandom Layered Existence page, retained as a concept index pending deeper manuscript citations.

title: O'Neill Cylinders
thumbnailAlt: A rotating orbital cylinder holding a ribbon of forest, water, and artificial sky.
summary: Orbital habitats where civilization rehearses how to build a world before it tries to repair or leave one.
description: The cylinders are more than escape pods. They are laboratories for ecosystems, governance, species design, logistics, and the old human temptation to mistake a controlled habitat for a solved world.
body: Their rotating interiors make gravity, weather, agriculture, and ordinary life into engineering problems with social consequences. Gold and Hephaestus matter here because a beautiful habitat still depends on an invisible supply chain.
The cylinders also form a bridge between the privileged orbital world that raised Grayson and the restored Earth he eventually tries to make possible.
sourceNotes: My Evolution System Fandom O'Neill Cylinders page; scale and timeline remain provisional where manuscript sources do not yet lock them.

title: Noble Kin
thumbnailAlt: An uplifted canine guardian standing at the edge of a recovering wetland.
summary: Uplifted guardians whose philosophy is summarized as force without ferocity.
description: The Noble Kin make restraint into a form of strength. Their role in the mythos is not to become perfect protectors, but to test whether power can remain precise when it is finally given a conscience.
body: Their culture treats protection as a duty and balance as an active practice. Intent detection, harmonic intervention, and pack coordination are useful only when they remain subordinate to judgment.

The species belongs beside the elves and the Web of Minds because it asks a related question through a different body: can a designed capability become a moral tradition rather than an excuse for control?
sourceNotes: My Evolution System Fandom Noble Kin page; this entry remains provisional until a manuscript source is attached.
